header.name: cache-control
header.info:
Postman added "Cache-Control: no-cache" as a precautionary measure to prevent the server from returning stale response when one makes repeated requests.

You can remove this header in the app settings or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: postman-token
header.info:
The Postman-Token header appends a random UUID to every outgoing request. Postman adds this header for API developers to better debug requests sent and to ensure separate requests appear distinct to the receiving server.

You can remove this header in the app settings.

header.name: content-type
header.info:
The Content-Type header is added to help the server identify the media type of the request body that is present in this request.

Use the request body tab to control the value or to remove this header.

header.name: content-length
header.info:
The Content-Length header was added to indicate to the server the size of the request body that is added to this request. Server uses this value to parse the request body accurately.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: host
header.info:
The Host header is added to identify the domain name for which a request is being sent to the server. This header is implicitly sent by every HTTP client.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value. It is most likely that without this header, your request will return an HTTP 400 error.

header.name: user-agent
header.info:
The User-Agent header is added to help the server identify Postman as the HTTP requesting application or client.

It is recommended that this header be sent, but you can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: accept
header.info:
The "Accept: */*" header is added to tell the server that Postman can understand and process all forms of response content types.

It is recommended that this header be sent, but you can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: accept-encoding
header.info:
The Accept-Encoding header is added to indicate to the server that Postman HTTP client supports a defined list of content-encoding or compression algorithms as response.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value. Doing that may not accurately render the response within the app.

header.name: connection
header.info:
Postman added the Connection header to indicate the server to keep the underlying network connection open once the current response is received. This allows Postman to reuse the same connection for faster response times in subsequent requests to the same server.

You can remove this header or enter a new one with a different value, such as `Connection: Close` to control this behaviour.

{
  "maternalLineages": [

request.description:
Uploads one document file for the authenticated user's enrollment.

Preconditions
- Requires Bearer {{accessToken}}.
- Enrollment must exist (POST /enrollment/start), consent must be accepted, and enrollment must remain DRAFT.

Request format
- Content-Type: multipart/form-data
- Field documentType (text, required):
  - Single-file types (new upload replaces previous file of same type): USER_PHOTO, BIRTH_CERTIFICATE
  - Multi-file types (every upload creates a new row): FAMILY_RECORD, FAMILY_PHOTO, ADDITIONAL_EVIDENCE
- Field file (file, required)

File validation
- Allowed MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, application/pdf
- Max size: 10 MB
- Empty file is rejected

Success response (200)
- message: "Document uploaded successfully" OR "Document updated successfully" (for single-file replacement)
- document: { id, type, fileName, fileKey, fileSize, mimeType, url }

Common errors
- 400: DocumentType are required
- 400: Unsupported document type: <value>
- 400: No file provided for upload
- 400: Invalid file type: <mimetype>
- 400: File size exceeds the 10 MB limit
- 401/403: auth/consent/enrollment guard failures

- 400: File size exceeds the 10 MB limit

Returns grouped document data for the authenticated user's enrollment.

Preconditions
- Requires Bearer {{accessToken}}.
- Enrollment must exist and pass auth/consent/enrollment guards.

Response format (200)
- Always returns an array with all document types:
  - USER_PHOTO, BIRTH_CERTIFICATE, ADDITIONAL_EVIDENCE, FAMILY_PHOTO, FAMILY_RECORD
- Each entry: { type, isSingle, documents }
  - For single types (USER_PHOTO, BIRTH_CERTIFICATE): documents is either null or one object
  - For multi types (others): documents is an array (possibly empty)
- Document object fields: id, type, status, fileName, fileKey, fileSize, url, verifiedByAdmin, rejectedReason, uploadedAt
- Multi-file arrays are ordered by uploadedAt DESC (latest first)

Common errors
- 401/403: auth/consent/enrollment guard failures

request.description:
Returns account and enrollment data for the authenticated user profile.

Auth
- Requires Bearer {{accessToken}} (JwtAuthGuard).

Top-level response shape (200)
- user: minimal user object from UserService.findById
  - id: string (UUID)
  - name: string | null
  - email: string
  - role: USER | ADMIN | MODERATOR
- enrollment: object | null
  - null when user has not started enrollment
  - otherwise returns the extended enrollment summary shape described below
- enrollmentStep: object | undefined
  - mirrors enrollment.steps when enrollment exists
  - map of step number to completion flag, example: { "1": true, "2": false, "3": false, "4": false }
- enrollmentStatus: DRAFT | SUBMITTED | APPROVED | REJECTED | undefined
  - mirrors enrollment.status when enrollment exists
- hasEnrollment: boolean

Enrollment object (when present)
- id: string
- status: DRAFT | SUBMITTED | APPROVED | REJECTED
- consentAccepted: boolean
- user: duplicated user summary inside enrollment
  - id, email, name, role
- personalInfo:
  - firstName, middleName, lastName, preferredName, maternalLastName: string | null
  - dateOfBirth: ISO date string | null
  - cityOfBirth, municipalityOfBirth, countryOfBirth: string | null
  - gender: MALE | FEMALE | NON_BINARY | TWO_SPIRIT | SELF_DESCRIBE | PREFER_NOT_TO_SAY | OTHER | null
  - pronouns: string | null
  - maritalStatus: SINGLE | MARRIED | DIVORCED | WIDOWED | null
  - occupation, educationLevel, specialSkills: string | null
  - languagesSpoken: string[]
- contact: { id, enrollmentId, email, phoneNumber, phoneType, allowSMS, createdAt, updatedAt } | null
- addresses: array of { id, enrollmentId, type, street, apartment, city, state, zipCode, country, yearsLived, createdAt, updatedAt }
- emergencyContact: { id, enrollmentId, fullName, relationship, phoneNumber } | null
- maternalLineages: array of summaries
  - id, fullName, maidenName, dateOfBirth, placeOfBirth, LivingStatus, approximateBirthYear, regionOfOrigin, familyOccupation, additionalNotes, relation
  - Note: key name is currently "LivingStatus" (capital L) in backend response.
- culturalConnections: array of { id, key, description }
- consent: array of { id, key, version, title, accepted, acceptedAt, required }
- documents: grouped document list (same structure as GET /document/list)
- steps: map of step number to completion boolean

Important integration notes
- When hasEnrollment is false: enrollment is null, enrollmentStep is undefined, enrollmentStatus is undefined.
- JSON object keys for numeric step map appear as strings on the wire ("1", "2", ...).
- Current backend throws generic Error("User not found") if user lookup fails, which may surface as 500.
